title: identity — settings
description: Identity substrate exposes NO tenant-configurable settings directly. Tenants tune authentication policy via `identity/auth` settings (password rules, session TTL, MFA requirements). This file registers the `identity` scope-consumer namespace with `stackra/settings` so future keys land under a consistent prefix.

namespace_registration.defaultValueFactory: Delegates to config('identity.*') when the tenant hasn't overridden. Ensures the effective value falls back to the config layer if no tenant-specific row exists.
namespace_registration.validator: Enforces the compliance-floor rules (COPPA / GDPR minor age) as a hard clamp: a tenant setting can only TIGHTEN password / lockout policy, never loosen below the compliance floor.

notes: Identity is a substrate module. Every user-facing setting (default MFA method, password rotation cadence, lockout duration) is owned by `identity/auth` — the module that presents the UI + evaluates policy at request time.
The one exception is the erasure hold-period, which compliance officers can extend per-Identity via the audit-logged CLI extension (see retention.json). That's per-row metadata, not a group setting.
When identity/auth lands, it will register groups under the `identity/auth` namespace, not `identity` — keeping the substrate module's namespace clean for future compliance-scope keys.
